description_long: This is a large, coiled brass wind instrument with a wide, forward-facing bell and a circular body designed to wrap around the player's torso. The surface appears matte and slightly tarnished, suggesting age or frequent use, with a muted silvery-gray coloration. Three piston valves are visible, positioned in a cluster along the lower front, with associated tubing and valve slides extending from them. The mouthpiece is attached to a gently curved leadpipe that feeds into the main body of the instrument. The bell flares dramatically outward, dominating the upper portion of the instrument and providing a strong visual focal point. The overall construction is robust, with visible joints and bracing typical of large brass instruments used in marching bands or street performances.
description: A large, coiled brass wind instrument with a wide flared bell, three piston valves, and a matte silvery-gray finish. The instrument is designed to wrap around the body and features a prominent, outward-facing bell.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full circular coil, wide bell, and the cluster of three valves with attached tubing.
description_view_1: Front view dominated by the large, flared bell opening, with the valves and tubing partially visible below.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the continuous coil, bell flare, and the rear structure of the instrument.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the bell and the circular body, with the mouthpiece and valves extending outward.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and dramatically flared, with a gradual outward curve that creates a large, open mouth.
attribute_valve_configuration: Three piston valves are arranged in a straight line along the lower front of the instrument, each with its own valve slide.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is gently curved, smoothly connecting the mouthpiece to the main body of the instrument.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: The main tuning slide is located just after the leadpipe and before the valve cluster, shaped in a U-curve parallel to the instrument's circular body.
